fire-alarm-monitoring.intro: Fire alarm monitoring links your premises detection system to a receiving centre so signals are actioned when the building is empty or staff cannot call the fire service. Icomply Property Services design, connect and maintain monitored fire alarm solutions across Greater Manchester and the wider North West, working to BS 5839 principles and your insurer’s expectations. From Stockport we help landlords, care providers and commercial occupiers choose the right signalling route, reduce nuisance traffic and keep logbooks inspection-ready.
fire-alarm-monitoring.body: Monitoring is only as reliable as the panel, power supplies and signalling path behind it. Our engineers survey existing loops, zone charts and ARC options, then commission dual-path or IP-based signalling where appropriate. We integrate monitoring with planned servicing so false alarms, line faults and battery warnings are investigated promptly rather than ignored. Multi-site FM teams can standardise call procedures, keyholder lists and weekly test records. Whether you need a first-time ARC connection, a change of monitoring provider, or remedial work after a failed audit, we quote fixed prices after agreement and leave clear certificates and handover notes for your compliance file.
fire-alarm-monitoring.meta_desc: Fire alarm monitoring across Greater Manchester & the North West. Icomply survey, connect & service ARC links with BS 5839 focus. Stockport engineers. Free quotes.
focus_points: Survey of panel, signalling path and ARC options with fixed-price quote
Commissioning of monitored fire alarms for commercial, care and multi-site estates
Integration with BS 5839 servicing, logbooks and keyholder procedures
Fault investigation for line failures, dual-path issues and nuisance traffic
faq: Do I need fire alarm monitoring for my premises?

Many insurers and fire risk assessments recommend monitoring when buildings are unoccupied overnight or have vulnerable occupants. We assess occupancy, category of system and existing signalling, then advise whether ARC connection is proportionate for your site.
Can you connect monitoring to my existing fire alarm panel?

In most cases yes, provided the panel supports a suitable signalling interface. We check compatibility, install or upgrade the communicator if required, and test end-to-end with the receiving centre before handover.
What documentation do you provide after fire alarm monitoring works?
You receive commissioning notes, updated logbook entries, signalling test results and any certificates included in the agreed scope, ready for audits, landlords and insurers.
fire-alarm-monitoring.seo_keywords: fire alarm monitoring, fire alarm monitoring North West, fire alarm ARC connection, monitored fire alarm Manchester, fire alarm monitoring Stockport, BS 5839 monitoring, commercial fire alarm monitoring, dual path fire signalling

Our team carries diagnostic kit, common spares and manufacturer knowledge for Advanced, Kentec, C-TEC, Morley, Hochiki and Apollo systems among others. Same-week visits are prioritised where capacity and access allow, with reactive attendance for faults that leave a building unprotected. After works we update service certificates, logbooks and zone charts so your next audit is straightforward. Use the form with your postcode, panel make and fault description, or ask for a multi-site package if you manage several properties.

We routinely cover 150+ towns from Stockport, including Manchester, Bolton, Stockport boroughs, Cheshire towns, Liverpool city region and into Lancashire and Cumbria subject to diary and site access.

Where capacity and safe access allow, we aim for same-week appointments and prioritise systems showing faults, isolation issues or failed tests.

periodic-fire-alarm-service.intro: Periodic fire alarm service keeps detection, sounders, power supplies and user controls reliable between major life-cycle upgrades. Icomply schedule and deliver BS 5839-aligned periodic servicing for landlords, care homes and commercial premises across the North West. From Stockport our engineers plan visits around occupancy, complete structured checks, and leave certificates and logbook entries that stand up to fire risk assessments and insurer reviews.
periodic-fire-alarm-service.body: A proper periodic service is more than a quick panel reset. We inspect control equipment, batteries, chargers, detectors and manual call points on a risk-based sampling plan, test cause-and-effect where required, and flag devices that need cleaning, replacement or re-siting. Findings are recorded clearly so responsible persons know what is advisory versus urgent. Contracts can align with quarterly, six-monthly or manufacturer-recommended intervals and can sit alongside emergency lighting and other life-safety PPMs. Multi-site clients get consistent reporting formats so estates teams can compare condition across buildings without digging through paper folders.
periodic-fire-alarm-service.meta_desc: Periodic fire alarm service to BS 5839 across Greater Manchester & the North West. Planned visits, certificates & logbooks from Icomply Stockport. Free quotes.
focus_points: Scheduled periodic visits aligned to BS 5839 and site risk
Battery, charger, device and panel functional checks with written findings
Service certificates and fire logbook updates after each visit
Optional multi-site PPM packages with consistent reporting
faq: How often should a fire alarm have a periodic service?

Most commercial systems need competent person inspection at intervals set out in BS 5839 and your fire risk assessment—commonly every six months as a minimum, with more frequent attention for higher-risk or complex premises.
Is periodic service the same as a weekly user test?

No. Weekly tests are typically a responsible-person check of a call point and indicators. Periodic service is a competent engineer’s deeper inspection, testing and documentation of the installed system.
Can you take over periodic servicing from another contractor?
Yes. We review existing logbooks, zone charts and panel history, then set a realistic service plan and catch up on overdue devices or missing documentation.
periodic-fire-alarm-service.seo_keywords: periodic fire alarm service, fire alarm periodic inspection, BS 5839 service, fire alarm servicing North West, fire alarm service Manchester, fire alarm service Stockport, planned fire alarm maintenance, commercial fire alarm PPM

Icomply offer BS 5839-focused maintenance agreements for single buildings and multi-site portfolios across Greater Manchester and the North West. Based in Stockport, we combine planned inspections with reactive support so facilities managers, landlords and care providers can demonstrate ongoing competence and control of life-safety systems.
fire-alarm-maintenance-contract.body: Contracts are scoped around system size, category, occupancy and access constraints—not a one-line “per panel” guess. Included visits cover structured servicing, battery health checks, device sampling and certificate issue; optional add-ons include emergency lighting co-attendance, monitoring liaison and after-hours response. You receive scheduled reminders, engineer reports and a clear remedial list with fixed prices before works proceed. If you already hold spare stock or preferred brands (Kentec, Advanced, C-TEC, Morley and others), we work with your specification rather than forcing unnecessary replacements. Tell us site count, panel types and current service history for a transparent annual proposal.

Certificates, logbooks and remedial schedules after each cycle

faq: What is included in a fire alarm maintenance contract?

Typical inclusions are scheduled competent-person visits, agreed testing scope, documentation, and defined response for faults. Exact cover—hours, spares and call-out rates—is written into the quote before you sign.
Can one contract cover several properties?
Yes. Multi-site contracts standardise visit windows, reporting and pricing across your estate while still reflecting each building’s system size and risk profile.
Do maintenance contracts include parts?
Labour for planned visits is usually included; detectors, batteries and major components may be quoted separately or bundled depending on the package you choose.

fire-alarm-loop-tester.intro: Fire alarm loop testing proves addressable wiring integrity, earth leakage and device communication before faults become full-system failures. Icomply use professional loop test methods during installation, commissioning and periodic service across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport our engineers interpret results against BS 5839 expectations and leave actionable notes—so electricians, main contractors and FM teams know exactly what needs fixing.
fire-alarm-loop-tester.body: Open circuits, intermittent earths and overloaded loops are among the most common causes of elusive fire alarm faults. We test loops during new installs, after building alterations and when panels report communication or earth faults. Findings may lead to cable repairs, isolator checks, device swaps or load rebalancing rather than speculative panel replacement. For projects, loop verification forms part of commissioning packs alongside cause-and-effect and battery calculations. If your contractor needs a third-party specialist to validate loops before handover, we can attend as the competent fire alarm partner with clear documentation.
fire-alarm-loop-tester.meta_desc: Fire alarm loop testing across GM & the North West. Icomply diagnose earths, opens & device issues to BS 5839. Stockport engineers. Fixed-price surveys.
focus_points: Addressable loop integrity and earth fault investigation
Commissioning-stage loop verification for new installs
Post-alteration re-tests after builders’ works
Written findings with prioritised remedial actions
faq: When should fire alarm loops be tested?
At commissioning, after significant building works affecting fire cables, when earth or communications faults appear, and as part of competent periodic servicing.
Will loop testing shut down my whole building?
Some tests require controlled isolations or temporary disablements. We plan with the responsible person to maintain life-safety cover as far as practicable.
Do you supply a report after loop testing?
Yes. You receive clear notes on measured issues, likely causes and recommended repairs so quotes for remedials are evidence-based.

The discharge itself lasts the rated duration (often three hours), plus preparation and restore time. We schedule around building use wherever possible.
Will testing drain batteries so lights will not work in a real emergency?
After a duration test, fittings must recharge. We advise on risk windows and can phase tests across large sites.

bs-5266.intro: BS 5266 is the key British Standard series guiding emergency lighting design, installation, testing and maintenance in the UK. Icomply apply BS 5266 principles on every emergency lighting project across Greater Manchester and the North West—from initial surveys to duration testing and certification. Based in Stockport, we translate the standard into practical site decisions for landlords, designers and facilities managers.

Does BS 5266 only apply to new buildings?
No. Existing premises are expected to maintain adequate emergency lighting; upgrades are often driven by FRAs, alterations or failed tests.

maintained-emergency-lighting.intro: Maintained emergency lighting operates as normal lighting or continuously lit signage and still provides emergency output on mains failure—essential for many exit signs and areas needing constant visual guidance. maintained-emergency-lighting.body: Maintained exit signs must remain legible, correctly oriented and unobscured by décor or stock. Maintained route lighting can also serve dual-purpose illumination in some designs. Service visits check both everyday operation and emergency transfer, because a sign can look fine on mains yet fail when the supply drops. Upgrading old maintained fluorescent boxes to LED often improves clarity and cuts energy while simplifying spares across an estate.

faq: Do all emergency lights need to be maintained?
No. Many route lights are non-maintained; exit signs are commonly maintained. Design follows BS 5266 and site needs.
Why is my maintained exit sign flickering?
Driver, LED board or supply issues may be to blame. We diagnose and replace modules rather than ignoring early warnings.
Can maintained signs run from a central battery?
Yes on central systems; self-contained maintained signs are also widely used. We specify the architecture that suits the building.

emergency-exit-lighting.intro: Emergency exit lighting guides people to final exits with illuminated signs and supporting luminaires when normal lighting fails. Icomply design, install and maintain emergency exit lighting across the North West to BS 5266 and practical fire safety expectations. From Stockport we replace faded legends, improve viewing distances and certificate systems for landlords, retailers and managing agents.
emergency-exit-lighting.body: Exit lighting is only effective if signs are correctly sited, oriented and supplemented by route lighting that reveals changes of level and fire doors. We survey existing signs for obsolete wording, poor contrast and battery failure, then install maintained LED exit fittings or photoluminescent solutions only where appropriate to risk. External final exits may need weatherproof luminaires so people are not discharged into darkness. Documentation links exit lighting into the wider emergency lighting system records rather than treating signs as decorative extras. Ask for a walkthrough if tenants report confusion during fire drills.
emergency-exit-lighting.meta_desc: Emergency exit lighting & illuminated exit signs across GM & the North West. BS 5266 install and service from Icomply Stockport. Free surveys & quotes.
focus_points: Illuminated exit signs with correct legends and siting
Supporting luminaires at changes of direction and level
External final exit lighting where required
Service, battery replacement and compliance records
faq: Do exit signs have to be illuminated?
In many premises illuminated emergency exit signs are required so they remain visible on mains failure; exact provision follows BS 5266 and the fire risk assessment.
Can you update old ‘Fire Exit’ boxes to modern legends?
Yes. We replace obsolete or damaged signs with compliant LED maintained fittings and dispose of failed units.
Should exit lighting be on a maintenance contract?
Recommended. Exit signs fail quietly; planned testing catches dark or dim legends before an incident.
emergency-exit-lighting.seo_keywords: emergency exit lighting, emergency exit signs, illuminated exit sign, fire exit lighting, emergency exit lighting North West, exit lighting Manchester, exit sign install Stockport, BS 5266 exit signs

escape-route-lighting.intro: Escape route lighting keeps corridors, stairs and designated paths usable during evacuation when mains lighting is lost. Icomply design and deliver BS 5266 escape route lighting across Greater Manchester and the North West, balancing lux levels, glare control and robust fittings. From Stockport we help commercial and residential blocks close FRA actions that cite dark stairwells or uneven coverage.
escape-route-lighting.body: Escape routes need consistent light at floor level, on steps and near fire doors—not a single bulkhead at the far end of a corridor. We survey routes, measure practical performance and specify LED emergency luminaires with the correct maintained or non-maintained mode. Multi-storey stairs often need careful spacing to avoid dark landings. After install we commission and set a testing regime so batteries remain capable of full duration. For occupied buildings we work floor by floor, maintaining safe egress throughout the programme and coordinating with fire wardens where required.
escape-route-lighting.meta_desc: Escape route lighting to BS 5266 across GM & the North West. Corridor & stair emergency lighting from Icomply Stockport. Design, install & test.
focus_points: Design for corridors, stairs and changes of level
LED emergency luminaires spaced for usable floor illumination
Integration with exit signs and open area lighting
Testing regimes that keep route lighting reliable
faq: Is escape route lighting different from open area lighting?
Yes. Escape routes follow defined path requirements; open areas cover larger spaces where people may need to reach a route. Many buildings need both.
Our stairwell looks bright enough by day—do we still need emergency lights?
Daylight does not replace emergency lighting for night-time or internal routes without borrowed light. We assess actual conditions against BS 5266.
Can you improve escape route lighting without full rewire?
Often additional or replacement self-contained fittings can upgrade routes using existing electrical infrastructure after safety checks.
escape-route-lighting.seo_keywords: escape route lighting, emergency escape lighting, corridor emergency lighting, stair emergency lighting, escape route lighting North West, BS 5266 escape route, escape lighting Manchester, Stockport escape route lighting

open-area-emergency-lighting.intro: Open area emergency lighting—sometimes called anti-panic lighting—helps people move safely across larger spaces to reach defined escape routes when normal power fails. Icomply specify and install open area emergency lighting across the North West for halls, open-plan offices, retail floors and similar spaces under BS 5266. From Stockport we ensure coverage is even enough to prevent panic without over-lighting or glare.
open-area-emergency-lighting.body: Open areas fail compliance when only perimeter exit signs are fitted and the centre of the room drops into darkness. We map furniture layouts, mezzanines and obstructions, then place luminaires to achieve suitable emergency illuminance. LED solutions reduce load and simplify battery maintenance compared with older gear trays. Systems are commissioned with records that distinguish open area fittings from pure escape route heads, helping future engineers maintain the design intent. For multi-use halls we also consider high-risk tasks that may need higher emergency levels when activities warrant it.
open-area-emergency-lighting.meta_desc: Open area emergency lighting (anti-panic) across GM & the North West. BS 5266 design & install from Icomply Stockport. Surveys, LED kits & certificates.
focus_points: Open area / anti-panic coverage design for large spaces
Even emergency illuminance to reach escape routes
LED fittings suited to halls, offices and retail floors
Commissioning records that preserve design intent
faq: Do small rooms need open area emergency lighting?
Small rooms may be covered by escape route principles or local design judgement; larger open spaces more often need dedicated open area lighting under BS 5266 guidance.
Can existing downlights be converted for open area emergency use?
Some luminaires accept emergency conversion modules if optical performance remains adequate; otherwise dedicated emergency heads are specified.
Will open area lights stay on all the time?
They may be maintained or non-maintained depending on design. We configure operation to match use of the space.
open-area-emergency-lighting.seo_keywords: open area emergency lighting, anti-panic lighting, open area emergency lights, BS 5266 open area, open area emergency lighting North West, emergency lighting Manchester, open area lighting Stockport

high-risk-task-area-lighting.intro: High-risk task area emergency lighting provides higher illuminance where dangerous processes or equipment must be made safe during a supply failure. Icomply design and install high-risk task area emergency lighting across Greater Manchester and the North West in line with BS 5266 principles. From Stockport we work with plant managers and duty holders to identify tasks that cannot simply stop in the dark—then engineer practical emergency cover.
high-risk-task-area-lighting.body: Examples include certain plant rooms, kitchens with hot processes, workshops and control points where residual hazards exist after mains loss. We assess tasks with the client, set appropriate emergency light levels and duration, and choose robust fittings that survive the environment. Integration with emergency stop strategies and fire procedures matters as much as lux figures. Testing regimes should prove that task lighting still hits the required level, not merely that an LED indicator is green. Documentation explains why higher levels were specified so future alterations do not quietly remove critical heads.
high-risk-task-area-lighting.meta_desc: High-risk task area emergency lighting across GM & the North West. BS 5266 higher-level task cover from Icomply Stockport. Survey, install & test.
focus_points: Identification of high-risk tasks with the responsible person
Higher emergency illuminance design where justified
Robust fittings for plant, kitchen and workshop environments
Verification testing and clear design records
faq: How do I know if I need high-risk task emergency lighting?
If a power cut leaves a hazardous task unfinished—hot oil, moving machinery, chemical processes—higher emergency lighting may be required. We assess with your team and FRA.
Is high-risk task lighting the same as normal escape lighting?
No. It typically demands higher illuminance focused on the task area, beyond basic escape route levels.
Can you add task emergency lighting to an existing system?
Yes. We can supplement existing escape lighting with additional heads or conversions targeted at the risk points.
high-risk-task-area-lighting.seo_keywords: high risk task area lighting, high-risk task emergency lighting, BS 5266 high risk task, plant room emergency lighting, task area emergency lighting North West, high risk emergency lighting Manchester, Stockport task emergency lighting

emergency-lighting-design.intro: Emergency lighting design turns fire strategy and building layout into a coherent plan of luminaires, modes, durations and test methods. Icomply provide BS 5266 emergency lighting design for projects across the North West—from Stockport surveys to installable layouts for contractors and in-house delivery. We focus on maintainable, inspectable systems that work for real buildings, not just perfect drawings.
emergency-lighting-design.body: Design packages typically cover escape routes, open areas, high-risk tasks, exit signs, system type (self-contained or central battery), and testing strategy. We coordinate with architects, electrical contractors and fire consultants where needed, and we flag constraints such as listed interiors or limited ceiling voids early. For refurbishments, design can phase coverage around occupancy. Clients who also appoint Icomply to install benefit from single-point responsibility from concept to certificate. Early design engagement prevents the classic problem of bulkheads added at the last minute without proper spacing or records.
emergency-lighting-design.meta_desc: Emergency lighting design to BS 5266 across GM & the North West. Escape route, open area & exit sign layouts from Icomply Stockport. Survey-led quotes.

Optional design-and-build delivery with certification
faq: Do you provide drawings for tender?
Yes. Design outputs can support tender or direct installation, with equipment schedules and notes for installers.
Can you redesign after a failed FRA?
We review FRA actions and existing kit, then design proportionate upgrades rather than unnecessary full renewals where risk allows.
Is photometric calculation always required?
Complexity drives method. Simple routes may be designed from established spacing tables; larger or critical spaces may need more detailed analysis.

emergency-lighting-commissioning.intro: Emergency lighting commissioning verifies that a new or modified system performs as designed before it is handed to the responsible person. Icomply commission emergency lighting across Greater Manchester and the North West with function checks, duration testing and clear records under BS 5266 practice. From Stockport our engineers close projects properly—so buildings are not left with untested fittings and empty logbooks.
emergency-lighting-commissioning.body: Commissioning includes confirming correct maintained/non-maintained setup, charge indicators, labelling, coverage intent and battery performance. We document as-fitted locations, raise snags for builder damage or missing legends, and brief site staff on monthly tests. For phased projects we commission each section as it becomes available while protecting overall escape provision. Main contractors value a single commissioning report that building control and clients can accept. If another contractor installed the lights, we can provide third-party commissioning only after verification testing of the installed works.
emergency-lighting-commissioning.meta_desc: Emergency lighting commissioning across GM & the North West. Function, duration & handover records to BS 5266 from Icomply Stockport. Book commissioning.
focus_points: End-to-end commissioning of new and modified systems
Function and duration verification before handover
As-fitted schedules, labelling checks and snag lists
User briefing for ongoing monthly tests
faq: When should commissioning take place?
After installation is complete and circuits are energised, before practical completion or occupancy of the affected areas.
How long does commissioning take?
It depends on fitting count and whether full duration tests run the rated hours. We programme realistic windows into the project plan.
What do we receive at handover?
Test results, schedules, outstanding snags and guidance for the responsible person to continue monthly function tests.
emergency-lighting-commissioning.seo_keywords: emergency lighting commissioning, commission emergency lighting, BS 5266 commissioning, emergency lighting handover, emergency lighting commissioning North West, commissioning Manchester, Stockport emergency lighting commissioning

periodic-emergency-lighting-test.intro: A periodic emergency lighting test is the scheduled check—function or full duration—that proves escape lighting still works between major upgrades. Icomply deliver periodic emergency lighting testing across the North West for landlords, care providers and commercial FM teams. Based in Stockport and guided by BS 5266, we run tests, record results and fix failures so compliance does not rely on hope.
periodic-emergency-lighting-test.body: Periodic testing usually combines client monthly function checks with competent annual duration tests, though higher-risk sites may need more frequent professional input. We diary visits, minimise disruption, restore charging and issue clear pass/fail data. Failures feed straight into repair or battery programmes with fixed prices. Multi-site portfolios can share a rolling test calendar so not every building discharges on the same week. Where self-testing fittings are installed, periodic professional review still validates that automation and physical condition remain trustworthy.
periodic-emergency-lighting-test.meta_desc: Periodic emergency lighting tests to BS 5266 across GM & the North West. Function & duration programmes from Icomply Stockport. Records & remedials.

Rolling multi-site calendars for estates teams
faq: What is included in a periodic emergency lighting test visit?
Agreed test type (function or duration), inspection of obvious defects, recording of results and recommendations for failed fittings.
Can tenants stay in the building during duration tests?
Usually yes with planning. We manage discharge windows and advise the responsible person on any temporary residual risk.
Do you remind us when the next periodic test is due?
Service clients receive scheduling support so annual tests do not slip unnoticed.

emergency-lighting-panel.intro: An emergency lighting panel—often a central battery control panel or test/monitoring panel—sits at the heart of many larger emergency lighting systems. Icomply install, service and fault-find emergency lighting panels across Greater Manchester and the North West. From Stockport we support central systems and advanced test panels with BS 5266-minded maintenance, clear labelling and duration test procedures for facilities teams.
emergency-lighting-panel.body: Panel work includes charger health, battery string condition, distribution MCBs, alarm indicators and any automatic test controllers linked to self-test or addressable emergency luminaires. We diagnose earths, failed sub-circuits and battery alarms, then leave the panel log updated for the responsible person. When panels are obsolete we design replacement central units or advise migration toward self-contained LED where that better fits the building. Combined visits with fire alarm panels are available for plant rooms that house multiple life-safety controllers. Provide photos of the panel fascia and any fault lamps for a faster remote triage before attendance.
emergency-lighting-panel.meta_desc: Emergency lighting panel install, service & faults across GM & the North West. Central battery & test panels from Icomply Stockport. BS 5266 support.
focus_points: Central battery and emergency lighting control panel support
Charger, battery and distribution fault diagnosis
Automatic test panel configuration and maintenance
Replacement or migration strategies for obsolete panels
faq: My emergency lighting panel is in alarm—what should I do?
Note indicator status, check if escape lights are affected, and contact us with the panel make and fault description. Do not ignore battery or earth alarms.
Do all buildings have an emergency lighting panel?
No. Many sites use only self-contained fittings without a central panel. Panels are typical of central battery or advanced monitored systems.
Can you service the panel under a maintenance contract?
Yes. Planned inspection, testing support and reactive call-outs for emergency lighting panels are available across our North West coverage area.
